summary: Reviewed the guest progress interpolation and host challenge-song detection changes. The main risk is that the guest display now recomputes reveal state from a local approximation instead of the host-authored reveal state, which can diverge in timing and configured extensions. Host challenge fallback improves coverage, but it relies on a loose text matcher whose safety is not proven by the pack.
appears_sound: Host challenge detection preserves intro precedence before challenge classification, so intro songs are not reclassified as challenge songs in app/host/[sessionId]/page.tsx:339.
Guest reveal calculation reuses the shared computeRevealState helper instead of duplicating threshold logic in app/guest/[sessionId]/page.tsx:203.
Guest interval cleanup is present for the interpolation timer, avoiding an obvious timer leak in app/guest/[sessionId]/page.tsx:60.

findings.description: The guest recomputes reveal state with only the base reveal config, so it ignores host-side extensionMs added to the active config. When the host extends a song, the guest can reveal metadata or show “Advancing to next song...” earlier than the host-controlled runtime state.
findings.evidence: Guest uses computeRevealState(interpolatedProgress, effectiveCfg) in app/guest/[sessionId]/page.tsx:203, while host applies prev.extensionMs to baseCfg before computing runtime behavior in app/host/[sessionId]/page.tsx:346.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If extensionMs is no longer user-reachable, the divergence would not surface in normal flows.
findings.what_would_confirm: Run or inspect the host extension control path and verify whether runtime.extensionMs can be nonzero during guest projection.

findings.description: The interpolation hook assumes one tick equals one second of authoritative playback progress from message receipt, but it does not use runtime.updatedAtMs to account for stale server state, delayed polling, or throttled timers. Reveal thresholds can drift from the host display, which conflicts with the project requirement that live clients stay consistent.
findings.evidence: The hook stores updatedAt in the anchor at app/guest/[sessionId]/page.tsx:43, but returned progress is only anchor.progress + tick * 1000 at app/guest/[sessionId]/page.tsx:64.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If runtime updates always arrive immediately after the host samples Spotify progress, the drift may be small enough for this projection UI.
findings.what_would_confirm: Add an E2E or manual test with delayed /runtime polling near reveal thresholds and compare guest metadata visibility against host runtime.revealState.

findings.description: The new challenge detector always falls back to loose title/artist matching after an ID miss, even when resolved challenge IDs exist. If challenge song data can be blank or very short, normal tracks may be classified as challenge songs and get the wrong reveal timing.
findings.evidence: detectChallenge falls through to matchesChallengeSong at app/host/[sessionId]/page.tsx:337, and matchesChallengeSong uses bidirectional includes checks without visible empty-string guards at app/host/[sessionId]/page.tsx:56.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: getChallengeSongs or setup validation may already filter out incomplete challenge songs, but that code is not included in the pack.
findings.what_would_confirm: Inspect getChallengeSongs/session validation or test a challenge entry with blank title or artist against a non-challenge playback track.

empty_categories: No new security or data-exfiltration risks found; the diff is limited to client display timing and classification logic.
No new CRUD or Supabase write paths were added by this diff.
No obvious new subscription or interval leak found; changed guest timers include cleanup.
